query: I want to write a new Claude Code skill that audits GraphQL schemas for over-permissive nullability. Walk me through the full eval-driven development process so I know the skill actually works before I ship it.
expected_behavior: Instructs the user to author the 3 eval scenarios (happy-path, edge-case, anti-trigger) BEFORE writing the SKILL.md body, not after
Specifies that each eval scenario must have exactly 3 rubric items written as third-person checkable assertions (not subjective items like 'response is helpful')

Describes a verification step where a subagent is dispatched per scenario with the SKILL.md inlined as context, the completion is judged against the rubric, and the skill is shipped only if pass thresholds are met
